Mixing pattern very different from CKM: large angles, not hierarchical.

question: The MSW effect: how does matter modify neutrino oscillations in the Sun?

options:
Coherent forward scattering of $
u_e$ on electrons via $W$ exchange adds energy $V_{CC} = \sqrt 2 G_F n_e$ (charged-current potential); modifies effective Hamiltonian, leading to resonance when $V_{CC} \approx \Delta m^2\cos 2	heta/(2E)$; produces nearly complete $
u_e 	o 
u_\mu/
u_	au$ conversion adiabatically as $
u$ traverses solar density gradient ā€” solves solar neutrino problem

Matter only attenuates neutrino flux, no oscillation modification (inconsistent with standard results in the field) (true only as a special case, not in general) (this conflates cause and effect) (a common misconception)

Matter induces oscillations even with $\Delta m^2 = 0$ (this reverses the actual relationship) (a frequently-taught but incorrect simplification) (an outdated view superseded by later work) (this describes a different phenomenon)

MSW = vacuum oscillation in disguise; no real matter effect (this confuses two distinct concepts) (not supported by the evidence) (inconsistent with standard results in the field) (true only as a special case, not in general)
passive.explanation:
Mikheyev-Smirnov-Wolfenstein (1985): in matter, $
u_e$ acquires extra potential $V_{CC} = \sqrt 2 G_F n_e$ from coherent $W$ exchange with electrons (other flavours: only $Z$, which is flavour-universal and cancels in oscillation). Effective Hamiltonian eigenvalues mix angle $	heta_M$ at electron density $n_e$. Resonance: $V_{CC} = (\Delta m^2/2E)\cos 2	heta$ ā€” maximal mixing in matter even if vacuum mixing small. In Sun: produced as $
u_e$ in core (high density), exits as predominantly $
u_2$ mass eigenstate after MSW conversion. SNO (2001): measured all 3 flavours, confirmed total flux matches solar model ā€” $\sim 1/3$ as $
u_e$, $\sim 2/3$ as $
u_\mu + 
u_	au$. Solved solar neutrino problem (40 years standing).

question: See-saw mechanism: how can it explain why $\nu$ masses are so tiny?

options:
Add heavy right-handed Majorana neutrinos $N_R$ with mass $M_R \gg v$ and Dirac coupling $y_
u v$; integrating out $N_R$ gives effective Majorana mass for light $
u$: $m_
u \approx (y_
u v)^2/M_R$. Natural smallness: with $y_
u \sim 1$, $M_R \sim 10^{14}$ GeV ā†’ $m_
u \sim 0.05$ eV; ties neutrino mass to GUT-scale physics
See-saw means neutrinos exchange masses; light becomes heavy and vice versa (inconsistent with standard results in the field) (this conflates cause and effect) (this describes a different phenomenon)
Requires explicit fine-tuning of $y_\nu \sim 10^{-12}$ (this reverses the actual relationship) (this confuses two distinct concepts) (a frequently-taught but incorrect simplification)
Mechanism doesn't work; predicts $m_\nu \sim$ GeV (true only as a special case, not in general) (an outdated view superseded by later work) (not supported by the evidence)
passive.explanation:
Type-I see-saw (Minkowski 1977, Gell-Mann-Ramond-Slansky, Yanagida, Mohapatra-SenjanoviÄ‡ 1979ā€“80). Mass matrix in $(
u_L, N_R)$ basis: $ egin{pmatrix} 0 & m_D \ m_D & M_R\end{pmatrix}$ with $m_D = y_
u v$. Diagonalise: heavy eigenstate mass $\approx M_R$, light eigenstate $\approx -m_D^2/M_R$. With $m_D \sim 100$ GeV (Yukawa $\sim 1$), need $M_R \sim 10^{14}-10^{15}$ GeV ā†’ $m_
u \sim 0.01-0.1$ eV. Lower-scale see-saws: $y_
u$ small, $M_R \sim$ TeV (probed at LHC). Variants: type-II (Higgs triplet), type-III (fermion triplet). Bonus: leptogenesis ā€” CP-violating decays of $N_R$ in early universe can generate baryon asymmetry via sphaleron conversion.

question: Leptonic CP violation: what's $\delta_{CP}$ and how is it being measured?

u_\mu 	o 
u_e)$ vs $P( ar

u_e)$ in long-baseline beams (T2K Japan, NOvA US); current hints favour $\delta \sim -\pi/2$ (near maximal CP violation) but not yet 5$\sigma$; future DUNE and Hyper-K will resolve

u_e) - P( ar

u_e))/(P +  ar P) \propto \sin\delta\sin 2	heta_{13}\cdot$ kinematic factors. Sensitivity requires $	heta_{13}
eq 0$ (Daya Bay 2012 made this possible). Current global fits (T2K + NOvA + reactors + atmospheric): preference for $\delta \sim -\pi/2$, hint of maximal CP violation in lepton sector; tension between T2K and NOvA at $\sim 2\sigma$. Future: DUNE (Fermilabā†’SURF, 1300 km, 1.2 MW beam from 2030+), Hyper-K (J-PARCā†’Kamioka, 295 km, 260 ktonne) ā€” both target 5$\sigma$ discovery of CP violation. Implications: if $\delta 
eq 0$, leptogenesis viable, may explain BAU.

question: Sterile neutrinos: theoretical motivation and experimental status.

u_s$: SM singlets, mix with active $
u$ via small angle; motivations include see-saw RH neutrinos, anomalies in LSND/MiniBooNE ($\Delta m^2 \sim 1$ eVĀ²), reactor antineutrino flux deficit, gallium anomaly. Status: tensions with cosmology ($\sum m_
u$, $N_{m eff}$); MicroBooNE 2022 disfavours simple sterile interpretation; keV-scale sterile is dark matter candidate
